heading: Governing body
label: 151904 Governing body
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)151904(a)36:607., §§ 7, 8, .Aug. 28, 1954, ch. 103668 Stat. 893151904(b)36:608.In subsection (a), the text of 36:607(a) is omitted as obsolete. In paragraph (1), the words “control of all funds of the corporation” are substituted for “control of all contributed funds as may be raised by the corporation” to eliminate unnecessary words.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)151904(a)36:607., §§ 7, 8, .Aug. 28, 1954, ch. 103668 Stat. 893151904(b)36:608.In subsection (a), the text of 36:607(a) is omitted as obsolete. In paragraph (1), the words “control of all funds of the corporation” are substituted for “control of all contributed funds as may be raised by the corporation” to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: The board of directors is the governing body of the corporation. Between meetings of the members of the corporation, the board is responsible for the general policies and program of the corporation and for the control of all funds of the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: the corporation shall have at least 15 but not more than 25 directors; and

children.label: B
children.text: at least four of the directors shall be members of the medical profession.

The officers of the corporation are a chairman of the board of directors, a president, one or more vice presidents as provided in the constitution and bylaws, a secretary, a treasurer, and one or more assistant secretaries and assistant treasurers as provided in the constitution and bylaws.
The manner of election, term of office, and duties of the officers are as provided in the constitution and bylaws.
